heading: Prohibition on discrimination in favor of highly compensated individuals
label: 300gg–16 Prohibition on discrimination in favor of highly compensated individuals
notes: Amendments2010—, amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 111–148, § 10101(d)“(a) .—The plan sponsor of a group health plan (other than a self-insured plan) may not establish rules relating to the health insurance coverage eligibility (including continued eligibility) of any full-time employee under the terms of the plan that are based on the total hourly or annual salary of the employee or otherwise establish eligibility rules that have the effect of discriminating in favor of higher wage employees.In General“(b) .—Subsection (a) shall not be construed to prohibit a plan sponsor from establishing contribution requirements for enrollment in the plan or coverage that provide for the payment by employees with lower hourly or annual compensation of a lower dollar or percentage contribution than the payment required of similarly situated employees with a higher hourly or annual compensation.”Limitation

Effective DateSection effective for plan years beginning on or after the date that is 6 months after , see , set out as a note under .Mar. 23, 2010section 1004 of Pub. L. 111–148section 300gg–11 of this title
amendments: Amendments2010—, amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 111–148, § 10101(d)“(a) .—The plan sponsor of a group health plan (other than a self-insured plan) may not establish rules relating to the health insurance coverage eligibility (including continued eligibility) of any full-time employee under the terms of the plan that are based on the total hourly or annual salary of the employee or otherwise establish eligibility rules that have the effect of discriminating in favor of higher wage employees.In General“(b) .—Subsection (a) shall not be construed to prohibit a plan sponsor from establishing contribution requirements for enrollment in the plan or coverage that provide for the payment by employees with lower hourly or annual compensation of a lower dollar or percentage contribution than the payment required of similarly situated employees with a higher hourly or annual compensation.”Limitation

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: A group health plan (other than a self-insured plan) shall satisfy the requirements of (relating to prohibition on discrimination in favor of highly compensated individuals).section 105(h)(2) of title 26

children.heading: Certain rules to apply
children.label: 1 Certain rules to apply
children.text: Rules similar to the rules contained in paragraphs (3), (4), and (8) of shall apply.section 105(h) of title 26

children.heading: Highly compensated individual
children.label: 2 Highly compensated individual
children.text: The term “highly compensated individual” has the meaning given such term by .section 105(h)(5) of title 26
